VOLT Portal — UX Craft PRD v3

Wave-5 requirements · 2026-07-21 · Author: Fable (architect) · Status: awaiting George's review — no packets dispatched until approved.
Companion for agents: docs/swarm/VOLT_UX_Craft_PRD_v3.md · Figures as loose files: docs/swarm/figures/ux-craft/

Contents
0. Verdict & method
1. The five mechanisms — what the old app actually does better
2. Global requirements (UXC-01 … UXC-12)
3. Screen-by-screen review
4. Non-visual defects found during the survey
5. Proposed wave-5 packet map
6. Open items for George

0. Verdict & method

George's read is correct. The old app (volt-timetracker.lovable.app) is a better visual instrument than the new portal today: easier to scan, better aligned, better separated rows, clearer information hierarchy. The new portal has won on data correctness, permissions, accessibility semantics, and honest states — and lost, so far, on layout craft. Wave 5 exists to close exactly that gap. No new features ship in this wave.

Method. Every routed screen of the new portal was rendered live (real components inside the real AppShell chrome, shipped fixture loaders, 1440×900, light theme) and screenshotted; the old app's capability-map captures are the reference standard. Screens with no old-app counterpart were reviewed against the same mechanisms in §1 — the mechanisms are the standard, not the old app's exact pixels. All figures in this document exist as loose .webp files in docs/swarm/figures/ux-craft/ so worker/checker agents can open them directly with the Read tool; checkers must verify against the figures, not against prose summaries of them.

Fixture caveat: numbers differ between chrome (mock: 337.8h/32 students) and page bodies (fixtures: small rosters). Ignore data magnitude; judge layout only. The old app's screenshots are dark-theme; the new captures are light-theme — judge structure, not palette lightness (both themes must pass every requirement).

1. The five mechanisms — what the old app actually does better

These are the transferable design mechanisms behind "the old app looks better." Every wave-5 packet cites these; every checker judges against them.

M1 · Fixed column grid

Every row is the same grid: expander · date+chips · title · PLANNED · EXPECTED/ATTENDED · actions. Each column starts at the same x in every row, so the eye travels vertically. In the new portal the stats block starts wherever the title block ends — a different x on every row (see §3, Outreach).

M2 · Three-tier type scale, column-locked

Tiny all-caps gray micro-label ("PLANNED"), bold value ("384h"), dim secondary line ("48h offered" / "Reached 40") — identical in every cell of the column. The new rows have label-over-value but nothing locks tiers into columns.

M3 · Compact affordances

A + expander, an EDIT chip, a red ×: three fixed-width controls at a consistent right edge. The new rows spend a sentence per control ("Show session details — Community Food Bank Sort", "View details — Community Food Bank Sort") — the largest single source of visual noise in the new lists.

M4 · Row separation & density

Bordered card per row; ~15 rows readable per screen. New portal: hairlines between tall rows, ~3 rows per screen, right half of most rows empty.

M5 · Color as encoding

Green = confirmed, purple = planned (stacked in one bar with a goal tick); orange = competition, green = outreach badges; per-team bar hues. The new portal renders every bar in the same default blue at full page width.

2. Global requirements — binding for every wave-5 packet

Each requirement carries acceptance criteria a checker can verify mechanically against screenshots and code. Severity tags say what a violation counts as in review.

UXC-01MAJOR if violated

Exactly one heading per section

The doubled-heading tic (section Heading immediately followed by the List component's own header repeating it) appears on nearly every screen: "Upcoming"/"Upcoming outreach events", "Next up"/"Next up", "Hours by team"×2, "Goal projection"×2, "Top events"×2, "Roster"/"Roster", "Sessions in July 2026"/"Chronological session list". Remove the duplicate app-wide: a List directly under a section Heading passes no visible header (screen-reader labeling via aria-label/aria-labelledby where the List API needs a name).

Accept: grep + rendered screenshots show zero adjacent duplicate section titles on every routed screen; screen-reader name for each list still present (a11y checker verifies).
UXC-02MAJOR if violated

Column-grid rows for event/meeting lists

Coach rows in Outreach and Meetings (and Calendar's session list) render on one shared CSS-grid row template: [expander] [date+chips] [title+location] [stat col ×2] [actions], with fixed/minmax column tracks so every stat and action starts at the same x in every row (reference figure A / M1). Stats right-anchored; title column flexes; truncation with tooltip rather than wrapping controls.

Accept: in a populated screenshot, a vertical line drawn at any stat column's left edge passes through the same column in every row; measured x-drift 0px. Checker verifies visually AND by reading the grid template in code.
UXC-03with UXC-02

Shared three-tier stat cell

One shared component (e.g. RowStat) renders micro-label (11px caps, dim) / value (semibold) / optional secondary line (dim, smaller) — used by every list row and dashboard tile so the tiers are identical app-wide (M2).

Accept: outreach rows, meeting rows, and secondary dashboard tiles all import the same stat-cell component; no local re-implementations left.
UXC-04MAJOR if violated

Compact affordances — never repeat the row's title in a control label

Expander: icon-only chevron/+ button. Edit: compact icon-or-short-chip. Cancel: compact × (destructive styling). Details: a short "Details" link. Visible control text never repeats the event title; the title goes in the accessible name only (aria-label="Edit Riverside Park Cleanup"), which preserves the screen-reader disambiguation the current design was trying to buy (M3).

Accept: no visible control label in any list row contains an event title; every such control still has a title-qualified accessible name (a11y checker asserts both).
UXC-05MAJOR if violated

One semantic color system

Define once (theme tokens): confirmed green · planned purple · goal-tick neutral; event-type badge hues (outreach green, meeting blue/purple, competition orange — matching the old app's instincts and the calendar legend the portal already ships); stable per-team hues for team-scoped bars. Apply to every ProgressBar/bar/badge. Verify Astryx's tinting API against installed source; if bars can't be tinted, wrap or compose — don't invent props. Both themes; contrast per UXD-09.

Accept: zero default-blue bars remain on Coach/Student/Parent/Outreach/Reports; confirmed-vs-planned visually distinguishable without reading text; token names documented in the theme file.
UXC-06MINOR if violated

Readable measure — content max-width and bar caps

Pages get a content max-width (~1120px; forms keep T125's 720px); full-bleed edge-to-edge inputs/bars/controls (Settings inputs, 1100px+ progress bars, full-width two-option segmented controls) end. Bars inside rows/cards cap near ~480px or the module's column width (reference B).

Accept: no interactive control or bar spans the full 1160px content region on any surveyed screen at 1440px.
UXC-07with UXC-02

Density targets and one separation system

Collapsed coach rows ≤ ~72px tall; ≥8 rows visible at 1440×900 on populated Outreach/Meetings; pick ONE separation system app-wide (bordered row-cards like the old app, or zebra + stronger dividers) and apply it to every list. "Session details" expander must not out-weigh row titles (fix the inverted hierarchy on Meetings).

Accept: populated 1440×900 screenshot shows ≥8 outreach rows; visual weight of any expander control < row title (font-size/weight comparison).
UXC-08MAJOR if violated

Goal/milestone strips are real components

The outreach "Team season goal" section currently renders "25% 50% 75% 100%" as disembodied floating text with no bar — it reads as broken. Build one shared milestone-bar component (track + fill + tick marks + % labels + compact stat row) and use it on Outreach and the CoachHome "Hours vs. team goal" tile.

Accept: screenshot shows labels attached to a real track with ticks; component shared (single import site count ≥ 2).
UXC-09MINOR if violated

Toast discipline

Milestone toasts currently sit on top of content indefinitely (CoachHome, Reports/Hours). Toasts auto-dismiss (~6s), stack in a fixed corner clear of content, and never obscure controls. Motivation-ethics unchanged (facts only).

Accept: toast region is position-fixed with auto-dismiss covered by a test; no screenshot shows a toast overlapping interactive content after settle.
UXC-10BLOCKER if violated

No internal jargon in user-facing copy

Found live in the UI: Settings renders "…a separate, not-yet-wired step -- module doc #2." Internal doc references, task IDs, and swarm vocabulary must never render. Sweep all user-facing strings; add the pattern to the copy checker's standing checklist.

Accept: repo-wide grep of rendered string literals for module doc, task-ID patterns (T\d{3}), "constitution", "packet" returns only code comments; the Settings string is rewritten to plain language (or removed if the limitation no longer holds).
UXC-11MINOR if violated

Friendly dates everywhere

Raw ISO dates leak in the edit dialog's Picked dates ("2026-07-26", "Remove 2026-07-26") and top-events rows ("2026-06-01 → 2026-06-03"). All user-facing dates use the app's existing friendly formatter ("Sun, Jul 26").

Accept: no YYYY-MM-DD literal visible in any surveyed screenshot after rework.
UXC-12George to confirm

Projection surfaces go chromeless

The kiosk QR page and the meeting Live Console render inside full app chrome — SideNav, season KPI strip and all — which is wrong for wall-projected surfaces and answers the parked kiosk question visually (see §3 figures). Add both routes to AppShell's chromeless set; Live Console keeps a minimal top bar (title, time, End meeting, Back).

Accept: kiosk and live-console screenshots show no SideNav/KPI strip; chromeless route test extended (T115/T123 structure tests updated legitimately).

3. Screen-by-screen review

3.1 Outreach list (coach) — the worst offender, and the wave's centerpiece

  • MAJORStats start at a different x on every row ("Planned" at ≈543px vs ≈487px on adjacent rows) — violates M1 → UXC-02.
  • MAJOR"Show session details — «title»" and "View details — «title»" sentence-controls per row → UXC-04.
  • MAJOR"Team season goal" milestone labels float with no bar → UXC-08.
  • MAJOR"Upcoming"/"Upcoming outreach events", "Past"/"Past outreach events" doubled → UXC-01.
  • MINOR~3 rows per screen; hairline-only separation; right 40% of rows empty → UXC-07.
  • MINORExpanded detail injects into the left column while the row's right side stays empty — expanded panel should span the full row width under the grid.

3.2 Meetings list (coach)

  • MAJORRow content is a single left-crammed stack; no columns (M1) → UXC-02/03.
  • MAJORInverted hierarchy: expander label bolder than event titles → UXC-07.
  • MINORFloating "1 canceled"/"…" cluster mid-whitespace — belongs in the actions column → UXC-02.
  • MINORDoubled "Upcoming"/"Upcoming meetings" → UXC-01.

3.3 Coach dashboard

  • MAJORDoubled headings on every module → UXC-01.
  • MAJORGoal projection lost the two-tone confirmed+planned encoding and the right-aligned numeric column of reference B; facts render as a per-row sentence → UXC-05 + packet-specific requirement (§5, W5-P4): two-segment bar (green confirmed + purple planned) with goal tick, right-aligned 64.5+76 / 90h · On track column. The Astryx "don't stack bars" guidance concerns multiple bars for one metric — two segments of one composite bar with distinct semantics is the correct reading; if Astryx's ProgressBar genuinely can't compose segments, build a thin custom bar (it's presentation, not metric math).
  • MINORSingle-column modules with 1100px bars; reference B pairs modules (hours-by-team ↔ top events) → UXC-06.
  • MINORSecondary tile grid wraps to an orphan row → even grid (4+4 or 3+3+2, consistent).
  • MINORPersistent toast overlaps content → UXC-09.
  • INFOKPI strip (season-wide) sits directly above team-scoped primary cards with no scope labels — add compact scope captions ("This season · all teams" / "Team: …") so adjacent numbers don't read as contradictory.

3.4 Reports (Participation / Hours / Events) — the app's best pattern, plus a messy toolbar

  • MINORConsolidate filter + threshold + sort into one compact left-aligned toolbar row → UXC-06.
  • INFOThe tables prove the codebase can do M1/M2 today — W5 list packets should crib this table's discipline.

3.5 Calendar

  • MAJORDay cells show no session markers at all — the calendar doesn't show the calendar's own content (Jul 8/22 sessions invisible on the grid). Add type-colored dots per day (uses the legend that already exists) → §4 D-C.
  • MINORVerbose links, doubled heading, full-width segmented control → UXC-04/01/06.
  • MINORDay-grid tracks stretch to 1130px with huge gaps; cap width or tighten tracks → UXC-06.

3.6 Leaderboard (student-facing)

  • MINORAdd proportional bars (per-team hue), team badge, %-of-goal secondary text; cap content width → UXC-05/06, §4 D-E. Anonymization (SEC-04) unchanged; motivation-ethics: bars state facts, no shame framing — same standard the old app already met.

3.7 Student home & parent home

  • MINORDoubled headings → UXC-01; bar caps + two-tone where confirmed/planned both appear → UXC-05/06.
  • MINORConsistency dots ~8px — enlarge to legible chips with weekday initials (both homes + StudentMeetingView).
  • INFOOld app's student view (figure below) is a tappable card grid — denser and friendlier for the check-off flow. The self-checkoff entry points shipped in wave 4; consider the card-grid presentation for the student past-events surface as part of W5-P3.

3.8 Standalone consistency strip (student meetings view)

  • MINORGive it a page frame (heading, padding, capped width, legible dots) or fold it into the meetings page for students → W5-P3.

3.9 Outreach detail

  • MINOREqualize RSVP columns, drop the underline styling on non-interactive name cells, cap content width → UXC-06.

3.10 Settings

  • BLOCKERInternal jargon in user-facing copy → UXC-10.
  • MINORContent max-width + section spacing → UXC-06.

3.11 Live console & kiosk (projection surfaces)

  • MAJORBoth surfaces belong in the chromeless route set → UXC-12 (George confirm).
  • MAJORStale banner copy contradicts the shipped codebase → §4 D-B; rewrite honestly (fixture mode is real until wiring lands) without internal jargon (UXC-10).
  • INFODoubled "Roster"/"Roster"; Check-in column oddly centered → UXC-01/06.

3.12 Reviewed and fine

Login (clean, tight card); the T125 edit-event form (sectioned fullscreen layout is now better than the old app's cramped dialog — only UXC-11 date formatting applies); Reports tables (per 3.4); Roster tabs (surveyed empty — real loaders + no fixture seam; chrome-level findings only: doubled "Roster"/"Students" heading under an already-selected Students tab → UXC-01; populated-state review is a follow-up once seams or live data exist).

4. Non-visual defects found during the survey

IDFindingDisposition
D-ASettings renders internal note "module doc #2" to users.UXC-10 sweep (W5-P1). BLOCKER-class copy defect.
D-BLive console still runs on fixture wiring (not-wired mutation seams) and its banners claim "no createClient/supabase-js usage exists anywhere in src/" — stale since T071 shipped the shared client. Functional wiring is real work beyond this wave's visual scope.Banner copy fixed in W5-P6; wiring logged as a wave-6 candidate task (needs Realtime + attendance mutations), not silently folded into a visual packet.
D-CCalendar day cells show no session markers.W5-P5 (visual feature, small: type-colored dots from already-loaded sessions).
D-DKiosk QR renders a placeholder — display-token Edge Function still unbuilt (constitution item 5 dependency, long known).Out of wave-5 scope; stays on the backlog with UXP/AUTH follow-ups.
D-ELeaderboard lost the old app's bars/%-of-goal/team badges.W5-P4.
D-FRoster tabs have no fixture seams → can't be visually surveyed populated.W5-P6 adds seams (test-only, same DI convention as every other page) + captures for a follow-up review.

5. Proposed wave-5 packet map (dispatched only after George approves)

PacketScopeRequirements
W5-P1App-wide mechanical sweep: heading dedup, toast policy, copy jargon sweep, ISO-date sweepUXC-01, 09, 10, 11
W5-P2Shared craft components: row grid template, RowStat cell, compact affordance set, milestone bar; applied to OutreachList as the proving groundUXC-02, 03, 04, 07, 08
W5-P3Apply the row system to MeetingsList + Calendar session list + student/parent surfaces (incl. consistency-strip page frame, legible dots, student card-grid consideration)UXC-02, 03, 04, 07 + §3.7/3.8
W5-P4Dashboard & Leaderboard composition: two-column modules, two-tone projection bars + goal ticks + right numeric column, per-team hues, tile grid, scope captions; leaderboard bars/badgesUXC-05, 06 + §3.3/3.6
W5-P5Page frames & small features: content max-width (Settings/Reports/Roster/Detail), reports toolbar consolidation, calendar day dots + grid tightening, RSVP column cleanupUXC-06 + D-C + §3.4/3.5/3.9/3.10
W5-P6Chromeless kiosk/live-console routes, honest banner rewrite, roster fixture seams + populated capturesUXC-12, D-B(copy), D-F

Sequencing: P1 and P2 first (P2 blocks P3/P4's row work); P5/P6 parallel-safe. Every packet's checker receives the relevant figure filenames from docs/swarm/figures/ux-craft/ and judges against them at the mechanism level — "directionally matching" is no longer a passing grade; the acceptance criteria in §2 are the bar. Color/theme work verified in BOTH themes.

6. Open items for George

  1. Approve this PRD (or mark up what to change) — packets only dispatch on your go.
  2. UXC-12: confirm kiosk AND live-console go chromeless (recommended: yes, both).
  3. Leaderboard bars (§3.6): confirm you want the old app's proportional bars + %-of-goal back on the anonymized leaderboard.
  4. Separation system (UXC-07): bordered row-cards (old-app look) vs zebra striping — default is bordered cards unless you prefer otherwise.
  5. Carried from wave 4: bulk "Mark event complete" parity decision (people-reached-only vs full per-day editing) — unrelated to this wave, still open.

Figures: docs/swarm/figures/ux-craft/ (32 files, old-* = reference app, new-* = portal survey 2026-07-21). Survey method and fixture caveats in §0. This document supersedes nothing — PRD v2's functional requirements remain in force; this PRD adds the visual-craft layer (UXD-05/09 continue to apply).
